entry.title: Five years of CPILint
entry.content:
On this day five years ago, I released the first version of CPILint on GitHub. I finished the latest version two days ago, so this feels like a good time to take a break, enjoy a piece of birthday cake, and look back at the project’s early days.

(The cake is a “Kagemand”, by the way, which translates literally to “Cake Man”. They’re served at birthday parties in Denmark, and they’re delicious 😋)

Version 1.0.0 came out on August 31st, 2019, but my earliest notes are from the autumn of 2018. They were jotted down between meetings during a visit to SAP in Walldorf. Seeing those notes today, I’m quite surprised how many of the early ideas eventually made their way into CPILint.

Reading the notes also reminded me that I originally used a different name: Flowmon. I think it was supposed to be a portmanteau of “iflow” and “monitor”, but it’s not a very good name, is it? I’m glad I changed it 😅

Even though I ended up writing CPILint in Java, I initially wanted to implement it in pure XSLT. I had a Schematron-like meta-programming approach in mind with a stylesheet compiling rules into another stylesheet, that would then transform an iflow’s internal XML format into compliance notifications.

I still have half a prototype, but the technique ultimately felt too limiting and I abandoned it. I still quite like the idea, though. It’s quirky and just a little chaotic 🙃 And it’s not a bad thing that it didn’t work out, by the way! On the contrary, one of the wonderful things about hobby projects is that they let you follow new ideas to interesting places and generally - in the words of Kurt Vonnegut - fart around.

The first time I showed CPILint in public was at SAP Inside Track Walldorf in January 2019. I managed to have a prototype working just in time for the event. Committing publicly to doing a live demo in front of a large group of people is quite motivating after all 🫠

SIT Walldorf was also the first time I got to show off the CPILint logo:

I was certain that someone would fairly quickly spot where the inspiration for the logo came from. However, five years later, no one has pointed out the connection. I’m a big fan of retro gaming, and the Atari 2600 console from 1977 is about as retro as it gets. This is the Asteroids game cartridge:

Look familiar? 😛 For the fifth anniversary I’ve made these logo stickers:

I’ll bring stickers with me to events in autumn, so if you’d like one, do come up and say hi!

Well, the “Kagemand” beckons, so let me wrap this up. Working on CPILint for the last five years has been so much fun, and I have no intention whatsoever of stopping here! The list of features that I want to implement is still very long, and I’m excited about bringing them to you in future releases.

Thank you for reading and thank you for supporting CPILint!

entry.title: Warning messages during transport import for "Import steps not specific to transport request"
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

 

We have noticed that long after the import was completed, the transport log was updated with a new section "Import steps not specific to transport request" that has several warning messages. It is mainly due to the mass object activation that ran in the background.

 

Can you please look into this issue and suggest us the next course of action ?

 

Thanks and Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Warning messages during transport import for "Import...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

Some import steps are not linked directly to a transport request. For example "Activate inactive runtime objects", "ABAP Dictionary Distribution" and "Feedback after export or import".

For example, during the step "Activate inactive runtime objects", the inactive nametabs are activated and there is nowhere a link to the transport that imported the DDIC change, this phase is listed under "Import steps not specific to transport request".

 

So the title "Import steps not specific to transport request" means that the system does not know to which request the steps belong to. The fact that many steps are listed in the "Import steps not specific to transport request" section of a transport request does not mean that all of them belong to this request.

When any kind of import is performed on the system (transports, Support Packages) a mass activation, distribution, conversion and movenametabs can be carried out on the system. The contents of the transport may be imported successfully but you may have outstanding conversions or activations related to objects independent of the transpor request. Until these inconsistencies are completed you will continue to see logs written under "Import steps not specific to transport" of all future transports. That is why it is written again to the transport log.

 

Kind regards.

Expectation is to be bring the sales GL group for that store against all the expenses of that store, so that a % of the expense against the sales of that store can be shown. (below table for reference)

Sales for this store in this month is Rs 500/-. Hence, it is expected that against all the expense GL's, the sales amount is displayed of Rs 500/-

GLStoreAmountSales%
ElectricityA10050020%
RentA15050030%
WarehouseA20050040%
SalesA500500100%

 

Currently, this is happening, by applying LOOKUP or RESTRICT or SUBTOTAL formula's.
All Formulas and Calculations | SAP Help Portal

System is bringing the sales values against only the sales GL account, but it is not fetching the same sales value for expenses such as electricity.

Hence, we are unable to derive the expense as a % of sales for that respective store.

GLStoreAmountSales%
ElectricityA1000#DIV/0!
RentA1500#DIV/0!
WarehouseA2000#DIV/0!
SalesA500500100%

Can you please let us know what way we can achieve this in SAC ?

 

Regards,

Wahid.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Double-Click Actions Not Recognized in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, I've tried double clicking in S/4 and found that I get the same result, i.e. a double is not recorded in S/4.  When I try double clicking in other applications, e.g. Word, I don't have an issue, the action is recorded correctly.  When I try recording other browser-based applications, such as Manager, double clicks are not recorded correctly.  So, I suspect that there is an issue with the browser extension or something similar.

Anyway, raise a ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and get support involved.  You will have to manually add double click actions to your Simulations at present, unless knows a work around. 

If you are concerned about the impact on content translation, use the translation capability provided through the integration to SAP Translation Hub, it also translates manually entered text.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Can not create temp file: Failed to open the connection. Database Vendor Code: 17002
entry.content:
Hi all, I'm run into an issue with a custom Spring Boot application that uses the RAS SDK. Here's what I can share:

Environments:

SAP BO

SAP BO: 4.3 SP4 which recently underwent an in-place upgrade from 4.2 SP

OS: Windows Server 2016 Datacentre

Local dev environment

OS: Windows 11 Enterprise 22H2

IDE: Intellij Ultimate 2024.2.0.2

JDK: openJDK 17.0.2

Spring Boot: 3.3.2

Crystal Reports SDK: 14.3.4.5014

Linux test environment

OS: CentOS 7

JDK: openJDK 17.0.2

Situation:

I’ve developed a Spring Boot application that:

  • Makes a connection to our CMS

  • Returns a ReportClientDocument with the provided CUID

  • Passes a single ID (Long) to a single parameter (“INVOICE_ID”) via a ParameterFieldController

  • Exports the report as a PDF to an InputStream using reportClientDocument.getPrintOutputController()

This code works as expected from my Windows 11 dev machine. The PDF version of the report is exported and downloaded to my browser.

When I run this code on the Linux environment, the application throws an exception when attempting to export to PDF.

Here’s the exception:

Can not create temp file: Failed to open the connection.
7984_16364_{1D957F0F-EE8B-4DA1-AB65-14DC86C1469F}.rpt
Details:  [Database Vendor Code: 17002 ]

Here are some code snippets:

ISessionMgr sessionMgr = CrystalEnterprise.getSessionMgr();
IEnterpriseSession enterpriseSession = sessionMgr.logon(reportsUser, reportsPassword, reportsCmsname,reportsAuthen);

ILogonTokenMgr logonTokenMgr = enterpriseSession.getLogonTokenMgr();
String logonToken = logonTokenMgr.createLogonToken("", 30, 1);  
return enterpriseSession;

//...

return reportAppFactory = (IReportAppFactory) enterpriseSession.getService("RASReportService");

//...

reportAppFactory = (IReportAppFactory) ienterpriseSession.getService("RASReportService");
//Get the reportClientDoc by CUID
IInfoStore infoStore = (IInfoStore) ienterpriseSession.getService("", "InfoStore");
IInfoObjects infoObjects = infoStore.query("Select SI_ID, SI_NAME From CI_INFOOBJECTS WHERE SI_CUID = '" + cuid + "'");
return reportAppFactory.openDocument((IInfoObject) infoObjects.get(0), 0, Locale.ENGLISH);

//...

ParameterFieldController paramController = reportClientDoc.getDataDefController().getParameterFieldController();
paramController.setCurrentValues("", "INVOICE_ID", ids.toArray());

//...
//This is where an exception is thrown when run from my Linux VM
InputStream pdfStream = reportClientDocument.getPrintOutputController().export(ReportExportFormat.PDF);

The REPORTS.CrystalReports2020ReportApplicationServer trace log (level HIGH) doesn't seem to give me much (from what I can tell). This seems to be the most useful, but I'm not sure what to do with it

ErrorLog 2024  9  1 15:52:49.816 7984 5520 (Administrator:34) (..\reporthandler.cpp:13139): CReportHandler::buildReportViewerError: CSResultException thrown.   ErrorSrc:"CRPE" FileName:"..\reporthandler.cpp" LineNum:13135 ErrorCode:756 ErrorMsg:"Failed to open the connection.

I’ve come across a few posts related to this error, but either have no solution or a solution that doesn’t solve my problem. I don’t think it’s a read/write issue related to to the SAP data and temp folders. I’ve confirmed that the SYSTEM account on my CMS Windows server has R/W to those folders. I’ve also tried using JDBC and ODBC connections with the report I’m working with, but does not make a difference.

The issue only happens from the Linux VM, so I’m guessing it’s something between that server and my CMS.

Any thoughts?

entry.title: Re: Can not create temp file: Failed to open the connection. Database Vendor Code: 17002
entry.content: Apologies, the code snippet was easier to read when I created the post.

entry.title: How to setup Solman monitoring and alerting on MII Schedulers?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Can you please let me know how to setup alert for SAP MII scheduler jobs. The jobs are stopping frequently. There was no Solman alert setup for that. It would be really helpful if someone provide the steps to setup the Solman alert for the scheduler Jobs.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to setup Solman monitoring and alerting on MII Sc...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Currently, in System Monitoring application we only deliver following standard metrics to monitor MII in technical instance template SAP MII (all versions)

  • MII DB query response time
  • MII connector response time
  • MII service response time
  • MII transaction response time

In case you want to monitor/alert on MII schedulers, maybe Interface and Connection monitoring is more in line with your requirement.

ICMon provides metric "Number of schedulers in critical status" which can calculate the number of MII schedulers in a critical status (PENDING or STOPPED).

Please follow the guide SAP MII Channel and configure the settings.

Best regards,

Ava Wang

entry.title: Re: Quota / Incorrect planning figures
entry.content:
1. Pls check the background configuration for Lot size, Normally the "EX" will not split the Quota.

And pls check the No splitting Quota scenario .

 

2.If you want to split the Quota,  you can create a new Lot size “ES”， and change the lot size of the materials, then it can split the Quota.

If the requirement Qty = 100, A vendor 20%, B B vendor is 80%.

Run MRP, the PR with A Vendor = 20, the PR with B Vendor = 80.

entry.title: 模拟物料需求计划（pMRP）
entry.content:
在我们生产计划业务中，时常会遇到产能瓶颈等，影响到对客户的交货承诺，SAP公有云ERP中的pMRP（Predictive Material and Resource Planning）功能可以帮助您基于可能的客户需求，通过模拟计划的方式，识别产能问题，并尝试找到导致产能问题的原因解决它。

计划模拟通常基于预测出的市场需求，在系统中，我们以独立需求的形式创建预测数量

然后去对您需要模拟的一个或者一组物料创建模拟计划

评估和处理pMRP模拟结果

 如果模拟结果是有产能超复合，或者物料货源问题（如没有生产版本等），在Demand Plan Simulation界面你可以看到各种问题的数量。

对于未找到生产版本的物料，创建好生产版本再重新scheduling pMRP simulation后错误消息会消失。

对于产能的问题，可以通过Issue list查看受影响的工作中心，以及超负荷的情况：

对于这些产能超负荷的工作中心，系统给出来了调整建议，比如将需求提前到有产能剩余的工作周，您可以采用这些建议，直接对需求数量和日期进行调整。

在Simulation Summary界面可以查看所有做过的变更，或者忽略的变更建议：

这些变更会在Release后对独立需求生效，也就是独立需求的数量和日期会在模拟计划Release后进行相应的变更。

这样，你就通过模拟计划实现了提前了解产品相关资源供应情况，在资源不足的情况下及时对生产资源或者需求计划做出调整，以更好的应对市场需求变化。

————————————————————————————————————————————
**本博文中涉及到的截图均来源于SAP内部测试环境。

entry.title: Re: Issue with Accessing Simulation in Published Version
entry.content:
Hi,

The published version of the Simulation URL should contain either /pub/ or /~tag/published/ embedded in the URL. For example:

https://anzdemo1.enable-now.cloud.sap/pub/Adoption/index.html?library=library.txt&show=project!PR_A2C5281E809B4D8D

https://anzdemo1.enable-now.cloud.sap/wa/Adoption/~tag/published/index.html?show=project!PR_7D5803DE0B07FC8C

If the URL that is causing the issue does not contain either string, it is the wrong version of the URL, i.e. the version for authors to check the content before publishing.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Enabling Web Assistant
entry.content:
Hi Aussie_Ray,

If the SAP Companion editing pencil button is not displayed at all, try embedding the following string into the URL of the application that contains SAP Companion, directly following the? in the URL:

help-editor=true&

For example, I can turn on the editing pencil in S/4HANA by amending the URL to look something like:

https://my304870.s4hana.ondemand.com/ui?help-editor=true#Shell-home

I didn't put the & after help-editor=true because there were no other parameter in the URL, the & is used as a separator if existing parameters are already in the URL directly following the ?

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Odata Service $skip vs. $skiptoken
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have checked this blog (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/difference-between-skip-and-skiptoken-in-odata/ba-p/13560047), and understood that the difference between $skip and $skiptoken is as follow.

$skip ->client-controlled paging

$skiptoken -> server-side paging

However, is there any difference when it comes to use-case in the reality?

For example, what I assume now is that maybe one of them would consume more data than the other. (I do not know if this is true.)

 

Could someone kindly advise on this?

 

Thank you.
